title: Hushkey
tagline: Property and jobs in Japan, surfaced in English

blocks.text: Japan has over 32 million foreign residents, visitors, and workers navigating a property and job market that was never built for them. Listings hidden behind language, agencies operating as gatekeepers, processes opaque by default. Hushkey surfaces it in English.

Two verticals, one market: rental and purchase property, and jobs — both sourced, translated, and made actionable for foreigners in Japan. Instant Japanese ↔ English translation built in-house, no third-party APIs. Free to browse, subscription-gated to apply.
Currently in active development. Dev preview at hushkey.dev — production domain hushkey.app held offline until V1.

items: Verticals: Japan property listings (rental / buy) and Japan jobs
Free browse · Dodo Payments subscription gates the apply flow
Operators are first class — local partners manage market content via the Organisations service
Branded mascot asset system, multi-pose, used through onboarding
Auth: Hushkey's internal auth class — same lineage as Hound and Howl. Hardened session model, MFA elevation for sensitive ops, no third-party identity provider
Translation: instant Japanese ↔ English engine built in-house — no Google Translate, no Claude API. Cached at write time, served alongside the listing
Search: prebuilt flat-JSON snapshot (~42KB) on R2, rebuilt via Hound cron
Maps: Mapbox autocomplete + geocoding + custom pastel style; nightly POI fan-out per property
Frontend: daisyUI page shells with Preact islands; React-ecosystem libs confined to ssr: false islands

rows: fly.io — Anycast routing onto designated regions per market
MongoDB Atlas (multi-region replica sets), no ORM — Zod + MongoService<T> (ObjectId/string id, optimistic lock, soft delete, ClientSession txns, LRU cache with versioned invalidation)
Hound (@hushkey/hound) on Upstash Redis — sorted-set delivery, cron, concurrency, WS streaming
Internal Hushkey auth class — same family as Hound and Howl
In-house Japanese ↔ English engine, no third-party APIs
*.api.ts files, Zod-validated, grouped admin / public / private / authentication / docs

items: Compute: fly.io — Anycast routes traffic onto regions chosen per market (Japan-first for V1)
DB: MongoDB Atlas with multi-region replica sets — failover and reads pinned regionally
Cache + queues: Upstash Redis with multi-region replication — Hound and the response cache share one tier
Split: API layer and Hound workers run as separate fly.io processes against shared Mongo + Upstash

blocks.text: OSS spinouts ship as Software — software.hushkey.dev (Hound and future tooling). The site you're reading is exactly that surface.

items: Solo founder — smallest viable slice; anything > 1 sprint gets decomposed
No third-party identity providers, no third-party translation APIs — both are internal
Bursty work always goes through Hound — handlers stay synchronous and predictable

blocks.text: Hushkey is in active development — entity Hushkey Pty Ltd (AU), dev preview at hushkey.dev, production domain hushkey.app held offline pending V1, contact leotermine@hushkey.app. License is proprietary; the OSS pieces extracted from it (Hound, Howl) live under software.hushkey.dev.
